title: Mobile Workbench with Drawer
summary: Add this beginner-friendly DIY workbench to your workshop with this weekend project.
description: Add this beginner-friendly DIY workbench to your workshop with this weekend project. It is a great sturdy starter workbench with a drawer and open shelves for storage and organization and can be easily customized.

steps.title: Build the legs
steps.body: Build the two sides of the leg frame by attaching the short aprons to the legs. Note that one side has 2 short aprons and the other has 4 short aprons. The extra short aprons will help support the shelves and the drawer slides. Use 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the long aprons
steps.body: Attach the long aprons to the bottom of the leg frames using 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue. Once attached to one side, attach to the other side. This will make the support for the bottom shelf

steps.title: Add bottom shelf
steps.body: Cut 3.5" x 1.5" notches in plywood using a jigsaw on all corners to accommodate the legs and attach to the frame using 2" countersunk screws or trim head screws

steps.title: Build the center divider
steps.body: Build the center divider as shown in the diagram. The boards are all joined using 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Add center divider to workbench
steps.body: Attach the center divider to the workbench using 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue. It is important to keep everything square as you attach.

steps.title: Add small shelf
steps.body: Cut out the 3.5" x 1.5" notches in plywood using a jigsaw on all corners to accommodate the legs and attach to the frame using 2" countersunk screws or trim head screws

steps.title: Add front support
steps.body: Using 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue, attach the support in the front as shown in the diagram.

steps.title: Attach top apron and supports
steps.body: Attach the top apron and the support brace using 2 1/2" pocket hole screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Build the Drawer
steps.body: Build the drawer using 1 1/4" pocket hole screws. The drawer can be made longer or shorter as needed.

steps.title: Attach the drawer front
steps.body: Attach the drawer front ensuring that there is a 1/8" gap on all sides.

steps.title: Attach the Workbench top
steps.body: Finally attach the workbench top to the top and support aprons using countersunk 2" pocket hole screws. Ensure that there is a 2" overhang on all sides.

steps.title: Done!
steps.body: Load it up with all your tools and get building!
